name: SU2
description: An open-source CFD suite used for aerodynamic analysis, optimisation, and research-grade aerospace simulation.
whyUseIt: SU2 offers serious CFD capability without enterprise licensing. It is valuable for learners who want to understand solver setup, mesh dependence, and aerodynamic optimisation in a real engineering environment.
fieldPosition: Aerodynamic simulation and optimisation in preliminary and detailed analysis
engineerWorkflow: Prepare geometry and mesh, define boundary conditions, run steady or unsteady cases, then post-process pressure, lift, drag, and convergence behaviour.
managerExpects: Validation discipline, mesh sensitivity checks, and a clear explanation of what decision the CFD run supports.
demoDescription: Run a baseline airfoil case at one Reynolds number, then test how mesh refinement changes predicted drag.

vocabulary.definition: The degree to which an iterative CFD solution stabilises toward a consistent answer.
A check of how much the result changes when the computational grid is refined.

beginnerMistakes: Using a single mesh and calling the result final
Ignoring whether the turbulence model matches the flow regime
